_doc: Phase 18 — Skill Prompts (no DB) registry.
Each entry under `skills` maps a routing skillId to a system prompt that
the chat router can pick at runtime. The router (detectIntent in server.js)
reads this file at boot, and again whenever an admin hits the reload
endpoint or restarts the server.
Required fields per skill:
id              — short slug, used internally (e.g. 'obsolete_check')
label           — human-readable display name (e.g. 'ตรวจคำสั่ง deprecated')
description     — 1-2 sentence summary — THIS is what the router reads
to decide whether to pick this skill for a question.
Make it specific. Bad: 'ABAP help'. Good: 'Detect
deprecated ABAP statements like INTO TABLE/INTO and
suggest modern alternatives.'
content         — the actual system prompt that gets injected as
additional_instructions on the Assistants run.
Paste the FULL prompt content from your OpenAI
Playground prompt here.
openaiPromptId  — optional, for traceability back to the OpenAI
dashboard prompt (e.g. 'pmpt_abc123').
Workflow when the ABAP team updates a prompt on OpenAI Playground:
1) Copy the new content from OpenAI.
2) Edit this file, replace the `content` for the matching skill.
3) POST /api/skills/reload   (or restart server)
When DB support lands (Phase 2 after PM approval), this file is the
seed data for the migration script.

skills.label: 🔍 Obsolete Statement Check
skills.description: Detect deprecated or obsolete ABAP statements (INTO TABLE/INTO single, MOVE TO, OCCURS clauses, INTERNAL TABLE without standard table type, etc.) and recommend modern replacements.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — PROMPT_OBSOLETE_STATEMENT

skills.label: 📋 SELECT Best Practice
skills.description: Review ABAP SELECT statements for best practices — FOR ALL ENTRIES handling, INNER vs LEFT OUTER JOIN choice, indexed field usage, SELECT * avoidance, ORDER BY without index.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — PROMPT_SELECT_BEST_PRACTICE

skills.label: ⚠️ SELECT in LOOP
skills.description: Detect SELECT statements placed inside LOOP/DO/WHILE blocks and recommend bulk fetch via FOR ALL ENTRIES or pre-loaded internal tables instead.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — PROMPT_SELECT_LOOP_CHECK

skills.label: 🔤 LIKE Operator Check
skills.description: Review usage of LIKE operator in ABAP — especially LIKE inside WHERE clauses, performance implications of leading wildcards, and CASE-sensitivity caveats.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — PROMPT_LIKE_CHECK

skills.label: 🧹 Cleanup Commented Code
skills.description: Identify commented-out ABAP code blocks (legacy debug, dead code, TODO leftovers) and recommend cleanup.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — PROMPT_DELETE_COMMENTED_CODE

skills.label: ✨ ABAP Best Practice (general)
skills.description: General ABAP code review covering naming conventions, modularization, error handling, performance, and modern ABAP idioms. Use as a catch-all when the question isn't specifically about one of the focused checks above.
skills.content: REPLACE WITH FULL CONTENT FROM OPENAI PLAYGROUND — ABAP_BestPractice_1
